summary: Matched two functions, `ifTime_CreateTimers` and `HSD_DevComARAMWakeUp`, promoted `src/melee/if/iftime.c` and `src/sysdolphin/baselib/devcom.c` to Matching in configure.py, and adjusted symbol metadata for related iftime data/string/float symbols. Review discussion centered on resolving an `@260` size mismatch through `symbols.txt` metadata rather than a C-side dummy static.

key_files.role: Matched `ifTime_CreateTimers` by reshaping locals and JObj digit traversal; review comments discuss an attempted static-data fix for a size mismatch.
Matched `HSD_DevComARAMWakeUp` by changing local lifetimes, buffer access expression shape, and ARQ/DC call arguments.
Changed `melee/if/iftime.c` and `sysdolphin/baselib/devcom.c` from NonMatching to Matching.
Adjusted related data, sdata, and sdata2 symbol names/sizes/types for iftime-adjacent symbols, including `@260` at 0x804D5790.

what_changed.item: Two units were marked Matching in configure.py: `melee/if/iftime.c` and `sysdolphin/baselib/devcom.c`.

`ifTime_CreateTimers` changed local declarations by adding a `digit` JObj pointer, replacing an unused `DynamicModelDesc** ScInfTim_scene_models` declaration with `void *anims, *matanims, *shapeanims`, staging `ifTime_match_timer_models` fields into locals before `lb_8000C07C`, and walking digit JObjs with `digit` instead of mutating `jobj`.

`HSD_DevComARAMWakeUp` removed a broad `buf` local, scoped `xfer_size` inside the type-3 branch, used `HSD_DevCom_804C6330_bufs[req_idx]` directly in cache/ARQ calls, and replaced raw pointer arithmetic from `devComStatus` with `HSD_DevCom_804C6330_bufs[req_idx]` for the zero-fill pointer.

`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` renamed several iftime-related symbols from semantic/address-style labels such as `ifTime_804D5788` and `lbl_803F94E8` to generated local-style labels such as `@152` and `@151`, and adjusted `@260` at 0x804D5790 to size 0x1 in the final diff.
The decomp-dev bot reported two new matches: `ifTime_CreateTimers` from 96.94% to 100.00% and `HSD_DevComARAMWakeUp` from 99.02% to 100.00%, with overall matched code +1884 bytes.

smart_moves.item: Used a separate `digit` traversal pointer in `ifTime_CreateTimers`, preserving `jobj` as the root/model object while iterating child/next JObjs into `ifTime_data.digits`.
Avoided a C dummy data object after review; the author confirmed updating symbol metadata following reviewer guidance about `symbols.txt` owning symbol address, size, and type.
In `devcom.c`, removed a reusable temporary `buf` and used the indexed relay buffer expression directly, narrowing local lifetime pressure and matching the reported function.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a build or diff reports a symbol size mismatch for generated data symbols, check and correct `symbols.txt` size/type metadata before adding dummy C storage.
For JObj traversal code, a separate traversal local can preserve the original/root JObj variable for subsequent semantics and produce matching code.
Direct use of an indexed buffer global, instead of carrying a broad temporary pointer, can be the matching shape for cache-management and ARQ request code.

naming_conventions.convention: Use role names for clear locals: `digit` identifies the child/next JObj traversal variable separately from the root `jobj`.
Do not keep semantic/address-style data names when evidence only supports generated local labels; this PR changed several iftime-related data symbols to `@NNN` labels.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Stage struct fields into temporary `void*` locals (`anims`, `matanims`, `shapeanims`) before a multi-argument call when direct field expressions do not match.
Narrow local declaration scope for values used only in one branch, shown by moving `xfer_size` into the `aramDC->type == 3` block.

Replace raw offset arithmetic with the known indexed buffer object when metadata/types identify the buffer, shown by replacing `(u8*) devComStatus + req_idx * DEVCOM_BUF_SIZE + 0x20` with `HSD_DevCom_804C6330_bufs[req_idx]`.
Use verifier output narrowly: the bot report names the exact functions that reached 100%, but the PR slice does not include manual objdiff logs beyond the bot summary.

review_feedback.feedback: Author reported a build error: `ERROR Size mismatch for @260 (type Object) at 0x804D5790: Expected 0x4, found 0x1`, and asked whether a proposed `static char ifTime_804D5790[4] = "";` was the right fix.
review_feedback.author: mchen91

review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer advised that `symbols.txt` contains symbols, addresses, associated size, and type; correcting the auto-size there should resolve the mismatch.
review_feedback.author: PsiLupan

review_feedback.feedback: Author acknowledged the advice and said the size was updated in the symbols file.
review_feedback.author: mchen91

follow_up_queries: dummy static char symbols.txt size type review
PR 2247 devcom iftime PsiLupan symbols.txt auto-size

notes: The PR body is empty, so intent is inferred from title, diff, bot report, and review comments.
The bot report provides validation-style evidence for two exact function matches, but the slice does not include standalone objdiff/checkdiff logs.
Review comments show an earlier proposed C-side static object fix, but the final loaded diff does not show that static in `iftime.c`; final evidence supports a symbols.txt metadata resolution.

The final diff changes `@260` at 0x804D5790 to size 0x1, while the discussion wording references a size mismatch involving expected 0x4/found 0x1; the intake records the final diff rather than inferring unshown intermediate commits.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Fix generated data symbol size mismatches in symbols metadata, not dummy C storage
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2247 review shows a proposed `static char ifTime_804D5790[4]` workaround for `@260` size mismatch was redirected to `symbols.txt`, where symbol address, size, and type are maintained.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Separate root and traversal JObj locals can help match JObj child walks
accepted_candidate_records.text: `ifTime_CreateTimers` matched after introducing `digit` for child/next traversal while leaving `jobj` as the original object variable.

source_update_candidates.title: Prefer symbols.txt metadata fixes over dummy C objects for size/type mismatches
source_update_candidates.text: When matching exposes a generated data symbol size/type mismatch, inspect and correct `symbols.txt` metadata before adding dummy static storage in C. PR #2247 review specifically redirected a proposed `static char ifTime_804D5790[4]` fix to symbol size/type metadata.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a standards-like rule supported by review feedback and final diff, but it should be curated into source standards rather than accepted automatically by intake.

source_update_candidates.title: `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` owns generated symbol size/type metadata used by iftime data matching
source_update_candidates.text: For this PR, reviewer feedback states that `symbols.txt` contains symbols, addresses, associated sizes, and types; correcting the entry resolved the `@260` mismatch discussed for `src/melee/if/iftime.c`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-specific fact for future data mismatch triage; requires curator review because intake should not promote path facts directly.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all `ifTime_*` data symbols should be renamed to `@NNN`; the PR only shows specific symbols changed in this diff.
Do not claim broad validation beyond the bot report's two named 100% function matches.
Do not treat direct use of `HSD_DevCom_804C6330_bufs[req_idx]` as a universal style rule; the evidence is local to `HSD_DevComARAMWakeUp` in this PR.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the written guidance that `symbols.txt` owns symbol address/size/type metadata.
